reviews.comment:
Quédate con el de las manos sucias y el corazón limpio…
con aquel que quizá no tenga lujos,
pero sí principios.
Porque hay personas que brillan sin necesidad de oro,
que sostienen sin necesidad de presumir,
que acompañan sin hacer ruido…
y cuyo verdadero valor está en lo que no se ve.

Quédate con el de la ropa sencilla y el alma valiosa,
porque en un mundo donde tantos se disfrazan de grandeza,
encontrar a alguien auténtico es casi un milagro.
Quien no necesita máscaras para ser querido
ni apariencias para sentirse importante,
es quien entiende lo esencial:
que lo que vale no se compra,
se demuestra.

Quédate con quien amanece cada día con una sonrisa
aunque la vida no siempre sea justa,
aunque las cuentas no cuadren,
aunque el cansancio a veces pese más que los sueños.
Hay un tipo de fortaleza silenciosa en quien avanza
sin tenerlo todo,
pero sin rendirse nunca.

Quédate con quien ensucia sus manos y cuida las tuyas,
con quien está dispuesto a trabajar,
a esforzarse,
a desgastarse si es necesario,
para construir algo que dure.
Con quien se preocupa por darte paz
aunque él mismo esté aprendiendo a sostener la suya.

Quédate con quien te ofrece todo,
incluso cuando no tiene nada,
porque esas personas —las que entregan desde la escasez—
son las que aman de verdad.
Son las que comparten el último pedazo,
las que se quedan cuando todos se van,
las que sostienen incluso con las manos temblando.

En un mundo que aplaude el brillo,
que idolatra lo superficial,
que valora más el precio que la esencia,
quédate con quien te mire con ojos sinceros,
con quien te hable con verdad,
con quien te cuide con ternura.
Con quien no sea perfecto,
pero sí presente.
Con quien no te prometa el mundo,
pero te ofrezca un corazón donde descansar.

Porque al final,
las manos limpias no siempre construyen,
pero las manos sucias con alma noble
levantan hogares, sostienen vidas
y aman sin miedo.

Y aunque la vida cambie,
y aunque el tiempo pase,
si encuentras a alguien así…
quédate.
Porque el valor de una persona
no está en lo que posee,
sino en lo que ofrece sin pedir nada a cambio
🧠🫀💫

(Translated by Google)
Stay with the one with dirty hands and a clean heart…
with the one who may not have luxuries,
but does have principles.

Because there are people who shine without needing gold,
who support without needing to boast,
who accompany without making a fuss…
and whose true value lies in what is unseen.

Stay with the one with simple clothes and a precious soul,
because in a world where so many disguise themselves as great,
finding someone authentic is almost a miracle.

The one who doesn't need masks to be loved
or appearances to feel important,
is the one who understands the essential:
that what is worthwhile cannot be bought,
it is demonstrated.

Stay with the one who wakes up every day with a smile
even though life isn't always fair,
even though the accounts don't balance,
even though exhaustion sometimes weighs more than dreams.

There is a kind of quiet strength in those who move forward
without having everything,
but without ever giving up.

Stay with the one who gets their hands dirty and takes care of yours,
with the one who is willing to work,
to strive,
to wear themselves out if necessary,
to build something that lasts.

With the one who cares about giving you peace
even while they are learning to hold their own.

Stay with the one who offers you everything,
even when they have nothing,
because those people—those who give from a place of scarcity—
are the ones who truly love.

They are the ones who share the last bit,
the ones who stay when everyone else leaves,
the ones who hold on even with trembling hands.

In a world that applauds glitter,
that idolizes superficiality,
that values ​​price more than essence,
stay with the one who looks at you with sincere eyes,
with the one who speaks to you with truth,
with the one who cares for you with tenderness.

With the one who isn't perfect,
but is present.

With the one who doesn't promise you the world,
but offers you a heart where you can rest.

Because in the end,
clean hands don't always build,
but dirty hands with a noble soul
build homes, sustain lives,
and love without fear.

And even though life changes,
and even though time passes,
if you find someone like that…
stay.

Because a person's worth
isn't in what they possess,
but in what they offer without asking for anything in return.
🧠🫀💫

reviews.comment:
Es la segunda vez que vengo a desayunar antes de las 8 hs
Te dicen que no tienen tostadas,me ofrecen tostados ,y no entienden muchos jóvenes,que trabajan en éstos lugares que no es un capricho,es claramente por un problema de salud/colesterol,no es la primer estación de servicio del acá,que nos pasa

(Translated by Google)
This is the second time I've come for breakfast before 8 am.
They tell you they don't have toast, they offer me toasted sandwiches, and many of the young people who work in these places don't understand that it's not a whim, it's clearly due to a health/cholesterol problem. This isn't the first gas station around here that's like this. What's happening to us?

reviews.comment:
Un desastre, era el próximo a cargar faltando 15 minutos para el cambio de precio ( 23:44 hs)y me dijeron chicos cerramos por cambio de precio cuando debería de hacer eso a las 00:00 y no solo eso el cambio de precio es automático, no lo hacen ellos. Para que mienten!!

(Translated by Google)
It was a disaster. I was next in line to load, with 15 minutes left before the price change (11:44 PM), and they told me, "Guys, we're closing due to the price change," when it should happen at midnight. And not only that, the price change is automatic; they don't do it themselves. Why do they lie?!
